name: pytest-mock-guide
description: Guide for using pytest-mock plugin to write tests with mocking. Use when writing pytest tests that need mocking, patching, spying, or stubbing. Covers mocker fixture usage, patch methods, spy/stub patterns, and assertion helpers.
capabilities: Apply the mocker fixture for per-test, class, module, package, and session-scoped mocking
Patch module functions, object methods, dictionaries, and context managers
Spy on real functions to track calls while preserving original behavior
Create stubs for callbacks and async functions with proper assertion methods
Use autospec to generate mocks matching function signatures automatically
Verify mock calls with enhanced assertion methods including pytest introspection
author: bossjones
securitySummary: All 152 static findings are FALSE POSITIVES. The skill contains only documentation for pytest-mock testing library with Python code examples. Pattern detections are triggered by: (1) Markdown backtick formatting being misidentified as Ruby shell execution, (2) Documentation examples of mocking OS functions like os.listdir for testing, and (3) Common method names like attach_mock being misidentified as C2 keywords. No executable code, network requests, or malicious patterns exist.
valueStatement: Writing unit tests requires isolating code from dependencies. This skill provides patterns for mocking, patching, spying, and stubbing in pytest using the pytest-mock plugin and unittest.mock API.
limitations: Does not provide actual test execution - guides on using pytest-mock syntax patterns
Does not install pytest-mock plugin - assumes pytest and pytest-mock are installed
Does not cover other mocking libraries like unittest.mock directly or mockito-python

useCases.title: Test Isolation
useCases.description: Replace database calls, API clients, and file operations with mocks to make tests fast and reliable.

useCases.title: Exception Testing
useCases.description: Simulate errors and edge cases by configuring side effects on mocked functions without triggering real failures.

useCases.title: Behavior Verification
useCases.description: Spy on functions to verify correct interaction patterns between components during test execution.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Mock Setup
promptTemplates.scenario: Replace a function with a mock
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to use mocker.patch to replace module.function with a mock that returns a specific value.

promptTemplates.title: Spy on Methods
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I use mocker.spy to wrap a real method while tracking all the arguments passed to it?

promptTemplates.title: Async Mocking
promptTemplates.prompt: What patterns does pytest-mock provide for mocking async functions and verifying await calls?

promptTemplates.title: Scoped Fixtures
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I use class_mocker, module_mocker, package_mocker, and session_mocker to share mock state?

bestPractices: Patch where the function is used, not where it is defined, to match how other code imports it
Use autospec=True or create_autospec to catch signature mismatches early in your tests
Reset call history with resetall() between test assertions when you need to verify specific call patterns
antiPatterns: Avoid mocking everything - focus on external dependencies, not your own well-designed code
Do not forget that patching builtins requires patching 'builtins.open' in the module that uses it
Avoid using assert_called() without arguments after multiple calls - it only verifies the last call

faq.question: What is the difference between patch and spy?
faq.answer: patch replaces a function entirely with a mock. spy keeps the real function but tracks all calls and return values for verification.

faq.question: How does pytest-mock differ from unittest.mock?
faq.answer: pytest-mock provides the mocker fixture that automatically cleans up mocks after each test and integrates with pytest fixtures.

faq.question: Can I mock async functions with pytest-mock?
faq.answer: Yes, use AsyncMock for async functions or mocker.async_stub for async stubs. AsyncMock supports all assert_await methods.

faq.question: How do I verify a mock was never called?
faq.answer: Use mock.assert_not_called() to verify the mock has not been invoked at any point in the test.

faq.question: What is autospec and when should I use it?
faq.answer: autospec creates a mock that matches the signature of the target function, catching errors when tests call mocks with wrong arguments.

faq.question: How do I mock properties or class methods?
faq.answer: Use mocker.patch.object for instance methods, PropertyMock for properties, and patch.object on the class for class methods.
